item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. Upvoted.

I do have a necro suggestion that the author will probably never see though :)

What if instead of being unable to pick the clover if one doesn't have the means for payment on them, the skip takes an equivalent item?

No cash, it takes money out of your bank account that you have a card for. No cards, it takes a shoe or something. Testing could escalate to where a d class with no fillings is completely naked and shaven and it takes about 4 dollars worth out of the body. Fat or blood or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, whoops! Can't believe that typo's been around for so long.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fixed a small inaccuracy: clover's genus "Trefolium" but "Trifolium". However, I didn't change the URL "www.trefolium4good.███" as I prefer to leave it to the author.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I like it, I enjoy when SCPs do nice things once in a while.

Anyway, here's wonderwall:

https://youtu.be/PUB8gIk1rjY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hell, I'd pick the clover. Very clean, the entire thing came across clearly, and I'd love to sign up for that newsletter. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Credit cards are a method of monetary transaction, so I assume they'd work.

White clover's scientific name is Trefolium Ripens, while red clover's name is Trefolium Pratense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When I first read this skip yesterday, for a while I simply didn't know if I liked it or not. So I didn't vote.

Upon rereading it today, I wonder what happened…!

A perfectly acceptable article, well-written and funny, full of unexpected surprises (OH NO, I thought, why money? Ruins everything!!!!! - Oh. Yeah. Charity. Of course. Okay).

I too would love to see some testimonies of people who couldn't pick up the clover because no money method of monetary transaction capable of transferring a value of at least US$4 (!!). And reactions from people at receiving the email… before application of amnestics… Tee hee.

Upvote.

EDIT: does it work with a credit card?
Oh, almost forgot: I keep wanting to see a pun in 'Ripens T. Pratense'… Does it mean anything or not? Cannot figure it out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad. The effect is somewhat amusing without being overly goofy, and the Foundation's response isn't something silly like engaging orbital lasers against known locations of four leaf clovers or whatever. The form e-mail thing is somewhat amusing as well. I'll upvote as a good example of what I imagined we would be getting as a result of the contest.

Unlike others, I don't think this needs extra stuff like people being punished for not picking the clovers or additional information on the charity. The former feels like silly moralizing, and the latter feels like forcing yet another GoI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, but I would have loved to have seen some "failure to pick" scenarios. Lightning strikes, spontaneous combustion… the possibilities for hilarity are unlimited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, I dunno. Are all four-leaved clovers part of this skip? Or is it just that any infected will have at least four leaves? The rest doesn't really grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I will upvote mainly because I find people losing their money for picking a four-leafed clover hilariously ironic.

On the other hand I am expecting to see more of this Trifolium Charities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So you went for nice!Foundation.

Pros:

  • I like the edits you made to the last portions of the procedures. It feels cleaner now.
  • The effect in itself is whimsical and that's cool.

Cons:

  • While the SCP is well described, there isn't much on the organisation behind it.

Verdict: Upvote (would like to see more of this organisation)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's actually a slight legal grey-area thing about using complete web addresses that you don't own, sort of like using a complete non-555 area code (or regional equivalent) phone number. One of those "There's no rule against it, but you can still get sued over it" things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1584 does the same thing, considering that the website got actually registered at one point. Easier to blackbox than not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
www.trefolium4good.███

Was that last blackbox really necessary? .com, .org, .gov can all be guessed to go there…

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Coming in at 471 words, I present to you all my Short Contest entry, and my first SCP in like 8 months.

Thanks to azzleflux for brainstorming with me, and to everyone else in the chat who critiqued this!

The picture of the four leave clover is by Valerie Giguere from Wikimedia Commons and licensed under CC-BY-SA 3.0. https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:4LeafClover.JPG
